title: Noun をよそに
explanation: Dùng để nói điều phía sau xảy ra bất chấp Noun hoặc không quan tâm đến Noun. Thường dùng khi hành động trái với kỳ vọng, dư luận hoặc hoàn cảnh xung quanh. Cấu trúc: Noun + をよそに.
explanationEn: The Japanese phrase Nounをよそに is used to express a situation where, despite some circumstance (represented by the noun), a certain action takes place. It can convey a feeling of disregard or nonchalance. In English, it can be translated as despite, notwithstanding, or regardless of
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を余儀なくされる
titleEn: Expresses the idea of being forced into a certain situation, to be compelled, to be obliged
structure: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる
structureEn: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる
explanation: Dùng để nói bị buộc phải rơi vào một tình huống hoặc phải chấp nhận một lựa chọn ngoài ý muốn. Sắc thái trang trọng, thường dùng trong tin tức. Cấu trúc: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる.
explanationEn: The grammar pattern Noun を余儀なくされる is used to describe circumstances where a person or party is compelled or obliged to do something due to necessity or unavoidable circumstances. It translates as to be forced to, to be compelled to, or to have no choice but to. This form is often used in formal or written Japanese
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を前提として
titleEn: Used to express on the assumption of, based on
explanation: Dùng khi lấy Noun làm giả định, điều kiện nền hoặc cơ sở để bàn tiếp. Có thể hiểu là “dựa trên tiền đề là Noun”. Cấu trúc: Noun + を前提として.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun を前提として is used to convey that something is done based on or assuming something else. It indicates a preconceived condition or assumption on which the following action or plan relies
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を前提にして
titleEn: Assuming; presupposing; on the assumption of
explanation: Dùng để nói một kế hoạch, nhận định hoặc hành động được xây dựng trên giả định Noun. Sắc thái gần với を前提として nhưng mềm hơn trong văn nói. Cấu trúc: Noun + を前提にして.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun を前提にして is used to show that something is done assuming or presupposing the noun that comes before it. In English, it can be translated as on the assumption of, assuming, or presupposing
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を境にして
titleEn: Means as a turning point, since (time), or from (time) on
explanation: Dùng để chỉ Noun là mốc ranh giới khiến tình hình thay đổi từ đó về sau. Noun thường là thời điểm, sự kiện hoặc độ tuổi. Cấu trúc: Noun + を境に(して).
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun を境にして is used to indicate a significant change or turning point marked by a specific event or time. It means that after this boundary, some situation changes or a new state begins
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を機にして
titleEn: To take the opportunity to; taking the occasion to; on the occasion of
explanation: Dùng khi lấy Noun làm dịp hoặc cơ hội để bắt đầu một việc mới hay thay đổi tình trạng. Có thể hiểu là “nhân dịp”, “lấy làm cơ hội”. Cấu trúc: Noun + を機にして.
explanationEn: The grammar pattern Noun を機にして expresses the idea of using a certain event or situation as an opportunity to do something else. It is often used when some change or new step is taken as a result of a specific event
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を皮切りに / を皮切りにして
titleEn: Used to indicate the starting point of some series of events or actions; starting with, beginning with
structure: Noun + を皮切りに / を皮切りにして
structureEn: Noun + を皮切りに / を皮切りにして
explanation: Dùng để nêu sự kiện đầu tiên mở ra một chuỗi sự kiện tiếp theo. Có thể hiểu là “bắt đầu bằng”, “mở màn bằng”. Cấu trúc: Noun + を皮切りに / を皮切りにして.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun を皮切りに / を皮切りにして is used to convey the idea of something beginning or starting with a certain event or action. This pattern is often used when the speaker wants to highlight the starting point of a series or string of events
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を皮切りにして
titleEn: Used to express Starting with, Beginning with
explanation: Dùng để nói Noun là điểm mở đầu cho nhiều hành động hoặc sự kiện diễn ra sau đó. Sắc thái thường gặp trong báo chí và văn viết. Cấu trúc: Noun + を皮切りに (して).
explanationEn: Noun を皮切りにして is a Japanese grammatical form used when one wants to express that something begins with a certain event or action, then continues on in a sequence. It is the same as saying starting with... or beginning with... in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun を禁じ得ない
titleEn: Cannot help but do, or inevitable
explanation: Dùng để nói không thể kìm nén một cảm xúc hoặc phản ứng trước Noun. Thường đi với các danh từ cảm xúc như 驚き, 怒り, 同情. Cấu trúc: Noun + を禁じ得ない.
explanationEn: The 〜を禁じ得ない (wo kinjienai) grammar point is used to express a situation or emotion that is so strong it cant be helped or is inevitable. It is used with nouns to express an uncontrollable feeling or action
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
